headline: Orang Asli healthcare access requires average 2-hour travel
context: A 2025 Health Ministry survey found that Orang Asli communities travel an average of 2 hours to reach the nearest healthcare facility.

cards.big: Orang Asli communities travel average 2 hours to nearest health facility
cards.sub: 215 villages identified as having critically inadequate healthcare access

cards.big: Health Ministry operates 98 mobile health teams for 853 Orang Asli villages
cards.sub: Mobile teams visit each village only 4-6 times per year on average

cards.big: Right to health under ICESCR requires accessible healthcare without discrimination
cards.sub: Maternal mortality among Orang Asli women is 3 times the national average

cards.big: Infant mortality rate in Orang Asli communities is 15.4 per 1,000 live births
cards.sub: National average of 6.7 per 1,000 highlights the disparity in health outcomes

cards.big: When healthcare is 2 hours away, treatable conditions become fatal ones
JAKOA proposed 12 new community health posts in 2025 budget submission
